observations: A mature pointed-eared humanoid leans over a long printed or ruled strip of paper while pressing a large wooden-handled stamp onto it.

Dark gray ridged skin, spiked black hair, facial seams or markings, layered scale-like shoulder pieces, wrapped bracers, and overlapping tabbed garment strips create a dense administrative-warrior hybrid.
The broad planted stance and both hands engaged with the stamp and paper make procedural control the immediate action rather than weapon combat.
The pale curling record is the only large light shape and pulls attention from the nearly monochrome figure to the act of stamping.
defects: Garment, skin, hair, boots, and shoulder pieces occupy a narrow dark gray-brown range, so torso layers and limb boundaries separate weakly from the background.
The stamp and paper explain an occupation or ritual, but no visible target, defensive behavior, or imminent attack establishes how the action threatens another character.
Repeated small rectangular garment tabs and crosshatched materials create even surface density without a strong secondary focal point below the hands.
uncertainties: The image does not resolve whether the stamped record enforces a supernatural effect, logs a transaction, or is ordinary bureaucracy.

observations: Three silhouettes show the same pointed-eared, spiky-haired figure stamping a long paper strip while shifting between a left lean, an upright centered stance, and a wide right lunge.
All three retain the same shoulder spikes, layered skirt tabs, boots, hand tool, and curling paper prop.
The center candidate most closely matches the master's mostly upright stance and centrally held stamp.
defects: The alternatives vary posture and paper curve but do not materially change body plan, prop class, garment family, or primary action.
The paper is rendered as unfilled light space rather than part of the black mass, so much of the role cue disappears in a strict outer silhouette read.
Without the paper interior, all three figures retain a conventional medium-height humanoid outline dominated by pointed ears and ragged coat tails.
uncertainties: The A, B, and C labels identify alternatives but do not mark which alternative was selected.

observations: The crop shows the head angled down toward the stamp with heavy upper lids, one eye more narrowed than the other, a lifted cheek, and closed lips pulled into a small asymmetrical smile.
The eye line appears directed toward the task or someone below frame rather than meeting the viewer, while the curved mouth suggests private satisfaction or calculation.
Pointed ears, ridged dark skin, etched facial seams, spiked hair, and large fingers create a mature nonhuman identity tied visually to the repetitive armor texture.
defects: The subtle half-smile and lowered gaze can read as mild amusement as readily as coercion or malice.
The hands and stamp occupy much of the crop, leaving limited evidence for a more specific fear, pressure, or emotional contradiction in the face.
uncertainties: Unaided inspection cannot distinguish whether the expression reflects satisfaction, smugness, concentration, or restrained contempt.

observations: At native size the upright pointed-eared humanoid, broad legs, forward hands, and pale curling paper remain distinguishable.
The paper is the dominant high-contrast role cue; the figure otherwise reads as a dark compact humanoid against a dark background.
The stamp shape, printed rows, facial expression, skin ridges, garment tabs, and layered materials collapse below reliable recognition.
defects: The act of stamping is not independently legible because hands, handle, and paper contact merge into one small cluster.
The dark clothing and body merge enough that the shoulder, waist, and leg silhouette becomes generic without the pale paper.
uncertainties: The thumbnail communicates a document-bearing humanoid but not whether the paper is a weapon, spell, receipt, decree, or ordinary record.
